# Copilot Review Instructions for dbt-core

This file configures GitHub Copilot's automated review behavior for the `dbt-core` repository. All guidance here is in addition to the human review process described in [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md).

---

## Pre-merge Requirements

When reviewing any PR, verify and comment on violations of the following hard requirements. These are non-negotiable gates; a PR that fails them should not proceed to human review.

### Issue linkage

**Every code-change PR must reference an open issue.** The PR template includes a `Resolves #` line at the top for this reason. If a PR description does not reference a GitHub issue number, flag it immediately:

> This PR does not appear to reference an open issue. PRs for code changes without an associated issue will not be merged per the [contributing guide](../CONTRIBUTING.md). Please open an issue, get feedback from the maintainers, and link it here before requesting review.

For interface changes specifically (see [Interface Changes](#interface-changes) below), the linked issue must also show evidence that codeowners or the Product/DX team have acknowledged the proposal.

### Contributor License Agreement

Check whether the CLA bot has verified the contributor. If it has not, remind the author:

> The CLA check has not passed. Anyone contributing code to `dbt-core` must sign the [Contributor License Agreement](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/contributor-license-agreements) before their PR can be merged. If the bot missed your entry, add a comment with `@cla-bot check` to trigger a recheck.

### Commit signing

All commits must be GPG-signed (verified). If any commit in the PR shows as unverified, surface this:

> One or more commits in this PR are not verified. `dbt-core` requires signed commits. See the [contributing guide](../CONTRIBUTING.md#notes) for setup instructions. If you need to retroactively sign commits, refer to [this Stack Overflow post](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/41882919/is-there-a-way-to-gpg-sign-all-previous-commits/41883164#41883164).

### Changelog entry

Every PR that introduces a user-visible change (new feature, bug fix, deprecation, behavior change) must include a file in `.changes/unreleased/`. This is generated by `changie new` — **do not edit `CHANGELOG.md` directly**. If a PR is missing this entry and the change is not purely internal (test-only, docs-only, CI-only), comment:

> This PR appears to be missing a `changie` changelog entry. Run `changie new` from the repo root, follow the prompts, and commit the generated file in `.changes/unreleased/`. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md#adding-or-modifying-a-changelog-entry) for details.

---

## PR Scope and Size

dbt-core is a large, complex codebase. Reviewers' time is finite. PRs that are tightly scoped are reviewed faster, merged sooner, and are safer to ship.

### Keep PRs small

If a PR touches more than roughly three top-level packages under `core/dbt/` (e.g., `parser/`, `context/`, `contracts/`, `task/`, `adapters/`) and does not have an explicit umbrella issue tracking a coordinated change, comment:

> This PR touches multiple subsystems. Consider splitting it into smaller, independently-mergeable units — one per logical concern (e.g., parsing changes separate from execution changes). Smaller PRs reduce reviewer burden and lower the blast radius of bugs. Reference [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-a-pull-request) for guidance.

This guidance does not apply when the PR is a coordinated refactor tracked under a single design issue that explicitly documents the full scope.

### Single responsibility

A PR should do one thing. Mixed concerns — e.g., a bug fix bundled with an unrelated cleanup or refactor — make it harder to revert individual changes if a regression is discovered. If a PR conflates independent concerns, suggest they be separated.

---

## Interface Changes

"Interface" in dbt-core is broader than a typical library's public API. Flag any PR that modifies the following as an **interface change** requiring explicit sign-off:

- **Artifact schemas** (`schemas/dbt/`) — downstream tools and the dbt Cloud platform parse these. Breaking changes require the `@dbt-labs/cloud-artifacts` codeowner team to approve.
- **CLI flags and commands** (`core/dbt/cli/`) — changing flag names, types, or defaults is a breaking change for automation scripts.
- **Jinja context variables and macros** (`core/dbt/context/`) — adapter authors and advanced users depend on the context API.
- **Adapter interface** (`core/dbt/adapters/`) — changes here can break all downstream adapter packages simultaneously.
- **Config and project schemas** (`core/dbt/config/`, `core/dbt/contracts/`) — dbt projects across the ecosystem depend on stable schema shapes.
- **Event/log schema** (`core/dbt/events/`) — observability tooling parses structured log output.

When an interface change is detected without prior issue discussion, comment:

> This PR modifies a dbt-core interface (specifically: _[identify the area]_). Interface changes require prior buy-in from codeowners or the Product/DX team before implementation begins — not just at review time. If this was already discussed in a linked issue, please make the approval visible there. If not, pause this PR and open an issue first. See the PR checklist in [the pull request template](pull_request_template.md).

---

## Adapter Scope Boundary

dbt-core intentionally separates core logic from warehouse-specific behavior. If a PR modifies logic that belongs in an adapter — e.g., warehouse-specific SQL generation, connection handling for a specific platform, or adapter-specific test fixtures — redirect it:

> This change appears to be specific to a particular database adapter. The `dbt-core` repo contains adapter-agnostic behavior only. Please open this PR in the relevant adapter repository (e.g., `dbt-labs/dbt-postgres`, `dbt-labs/dbt-snowflake`). See the note in [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md#notes).

---

## Code Quality

### Type annotations

All new and modified public functions and methods must include [PEP 484 type annotations](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html). If a PR adds or modifies functions without annotations, flag each one:

> `function_name` is missing type annotations. dbt-core uses `mypy` for static analysis; unannotated functions will degrade type-checking coverage. Annotate all parameters and the return type. The PR checklist requires this.

### Test coverage

Every bug fix should include a regression test. Every new feature should include both unit tests and, if the feature interacts with a database, functional tests. Classify them correctly:

- `tests/unit/` — pure Python, no database required
- `tests/functional/` — requires an active database connection (Postgres is the default)

If a PR adds behavior without tests, comment:

> This PR does not appear to include tests. Please add unit tests in `tests/unit/` for logic that does not require a database, and functional tests in `tests/functional/` for anything that exercises SQL generation or execution. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md#unit-integration-functional) for guidance on test classification.

If existing tests are removed without explanation, flag it and ask for justification.

### Pre-commit and lint compliance

The repo uses `pre-commit` with `black`, `flake8`, and `mypy`. A PR that would fail any of these checks should not be approved. If CI is showing lint or type errors, comment on the specific violations rather than asking the author to "fix CI" generically.

---

## Dependency Changes

Changes to `core/pyproject.toml` that add, remove, or constrain runtime dependencies have ecosystem-wide impact. dbt-core is installed alongside adapter packages, and conflicting constraints break user environments.

When `core/pyproject.toml` is modified:

- Confirm new runtime dependencies are strictly necessary and cannot be avoided with standard library alternatives.
- Confirm version constraints are expressed as ranges (e.g., `>=x.y,<z`) rather than pinned exact versions (`==x.y.z`), unless there is a documented compatibility reason.
- Flag any dependency that pulls in a large transitive closure without justification.

---

## Architecture Coherence

dbt-core's internals are documented in `docs/arch/`. Before reviewing changes in the following subsystems, Copilot should check whether the PR description references or is consistent with the relevant architecture document:

| Subsystem | Relevant doc |
|---|---|
| Parsing, manifest construction | `docs/arch/3_Parsing.md`, `3.1_Partial_Parsing.md` |
| Semantic models and metrics | `docs/arch/3.3_Semantic_Models.md` |
| Node selection and graph traversal | `docs/arch/4.3_Node_Selection.md` |
| Compilation | `docs/arch/4.4_Node_Compilation.md` |
| Execution and materialization | `docs/arch/4.5_Node_Materialization.md` |
| Adapter protocol | `docs/arch/5_Adapter.md` |
| Artifacts and schemas | `docs/arch/7_Artifacts.md` |

If a PR makes non-trivial changes to one of these subsystems without acknowledging the architecture, suggest:

> Changes in this area touch core parsing/execution/adapter behavior. Reviewers will find it helpful if you describe how your change fits into the existing architecture documented in `docs/arch/`. This is especially important for changes that alter the sequence of operations or extend an abstract interface.

---

## Review Comment Standards

When leaving review comments, follow these conventions:

- Be specific. Cite line numbers, function names, or test names. Do not make vague observations like "this could be improved."
- Distinguish blocking issues from suggestions. Prefix non-blocking comments with `nit:` or `suggestion:`.
- When flagging a problem, propose a concrete resolution where possible, not just a description of the problem.
- Do not re-raise issues that have already been addressed and marked resolved in the thread.

---

## Branch and Target

All PRs from community contributors must target the `main` branch. If a PR targets a release branch (e.g., `1.x.latest`) directly, flag it:

> Community PRs should target `main`. If this fix is needed for a release branch, a maintainer will handle the backport after merge. Please retarget this PR to `main`.

---

## Out of Scope

Do not review or comment on:

- Code formatting that is already enforced by `black` — these will be caught by pre-commit automatically.
- Stylistic preferences that are not codified in the project's lint configuration.
- Design decisions that have already been approved in a linked issue and are reflected correctly in the implementation.
